description: A heart rate study dataset is loaded in JASP (file: '/home/ga/Documents/JASP/HeartRate.csv'). This dataset contains 800 observations from a study comparing resting heart rates between runners and sedentary controls, with participants of both genders. The three columns are: 'Gender' (Female/Male), 'Group' (Control/Runners), and 'Heart Rate' (beats per minute). The researchers suspect that the heart rate data may not meet parametric assumptions and want nonparametric analyses. Perform a Kruskal-Wallis test with 'Heart Rate' as the dependent variable and 'Group' as the grouping variable, including descriptive statistics. Conduct a Mann-Whitney U test comparing 'Heart Rate' between 'Gender' groups, including descriptive statistics and the effect size. Generate descriptive statistics for 'Heart Rate' split by 'Group', including boxplots and distribution plots. Save the completed analysis file as '/home/ga/Documents/JASP/heart_rate_nonparametric.jasp'.
